none: FAP-3, Milwaukee County expressway system construction to connect I-94 stadium interchange and I-894 Greenfield interchange, Milwaukee County : environmental impact statement.

This document details the construction of the Stadium Freeway project in Milwaukee, Wisconsin. It outlines the project's goal of creating a continuous, landscaped corridor connecting the city's major areas, including the Stadium, Alverno College, and the Kinnickinnic River Parkway. The project involves collaboration between the Wisconsin Department of Transportation, the Milwaukee County ExPressway and Transportation Commission, the Milwaukee Public Schools, the City of Milwaukee, and the Milwaukee County Park Commission.  The document highlights specific design elements, including landscaping, tree planting, and the integration of the Kinnickinnic River channel.  It also details coordination efforts with the City of Greenfield and School District No. 8, addressing concerns related to park land acquisition and the implementation of the freeway's design to minimize negative impacts on the park.
FAP-3, Milwaukee County expressway system construction to connect I-94 stadium interchange and I-894 Greenfield interchange, Milwaukee County : environmental impact statement.

It outlines the project's goal of creating a continuous, landscaped corridor connecting the city's major areas, including the Stadium, Alverno College, and the Kinnickinnic River Parkway. The project involves collaboration between the Wisconsin Department of Transportation, the Milwaukee County ExPressway and Transportation Commission, the Milwaukee Public Schools, the City of Milwaukee, and the Milwaukee County Park Commission.  The document highlights specific design elements, including landscaping, tree planting, and the integration of the Kinnickinnic River channel. John Hibbs; I. C. Herried; Elmer Krieger

The Stadium Freeway project, initiated in the 1960s, was a major infrastructure development undertaken by the Milwaukee County government to alleviate traffic congestion and improve transportation options in the region. It was a significant undertaking, involving extensive land acquisition and environmental assessments, and faced considerable public opposition. Ultimately, the project was completed, though with some modifications and adjustments to the design, reflecting the challenges of integrating the freeway with existing urban landscapes and community concerns. It was deemed a success in terms of traffic flow improvements, but also generated considerable debate regarding its impact on the surrounding environment and community character.
